vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or entirely obscured by darkness. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but shows no visible foliage, only the anchor rabbit.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Anchor rabbit visible, plant foliage absent/dormant)

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only dark, featureless soil.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains only dark, featureless soil.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains the white rabbit anchor; no green biomass detected.
plant_audit.P4: Shared black pot with P2; no distinct foliage detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially desiccated across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: A metal spoon appears in the T-1 image, representing a non-botanical intervention.

temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Loss of visibility due to lighting conditions; no growth observed.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Introduction of a metal spoon in the yellow pot (P1).
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Removal of the spoon; return to baseline darkness.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure or total dormancy. There is zero evidence of photosynthetic activity or viable plant structure in any of the pots.
anomalies: Total absence of expected foliage across all registered pots
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete lack of visible plant life. The environment is dominated by dark, unidentifiable soil. The white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only consistent landmark. The brief appearance of a spoon suggests human interaction, but no botanical progress has been made. The biome is currently non-functional.
